existing_Entity_Description: Prince George is a municipality in Northern BC - Prince George, British Columbia, Canada, within the Fraser-Fort George. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Prince George had a population of 76,708. The community is situated in the hub of Northern BC — a gateway city serving a vast northern territory. a regional service centre with low competition and high opportunity. The region features cold winters and warm summers with significant seasonal temperature swings.

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Continental northern climate: long cold winters to -30°C, short warm summers; deep frost, ice damming and snow-load engineering are standard; spring breakup roughens roads and sites.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: Rolling Interior Plateau forest at the confluence of the Fraser and Nechako rivers; Prince George is the hub of a vast forested hinterland.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Prince George is northern BC's capital in all but name: forestry and pulp mills, the University of Northern British Columbia (UNBC), regional healthcare, and CN Rail's junction of north-south and east-west lines.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Post-war and 1970s-boom houses built for deep winter, newer subdivisions on the city's benches, and rural properties on large lots outside town.
